activity_name: Record the data of the patient

substeps.description: Open the Hospital Information System (HIS) and navigate to the patient registration module.
Select the option to create a new patient record.
Enter the patient's basic demographic information including date of birth, gender, address, and contact number.
Verify the entered information for accuracy and save the initial record.

activity_name: Register the SSN of the patient

substeps.description: Ask the patient for their Social Security Number (SSN) and request to see their Social Security card or official document.
Enter the SSN into the designated field in the HIS.
Re-enter the SSN to confirm accuracy and validate the entry against the document.
Save the SSN record in the patient's profile.

activity_name: Register the Name of the patient

substeps.description: Request the patient's official government-issued photo ID (e.g., driver's license or passport).
Locate the patient's legal first name and middle name(s) on the ID.
Type the first name and middle name(s) exactly as shown on the ID into the corresponding fields in the HIS.
Review the entered name for typos and confirm with the patient before saving.

activity_name: Register the Surname of the patient

substeps.description: Refer to the patient's official ID to identify the legal surname (family name).
Enter the surname exactly as printed on the ID into the surname field in the HIS.
Double-check the spelling against the ID and ask the patient to confirm.
Save the surname entry to the patient record.
Navigate to the registration confirmation or referral document template in the HIS.
Select the patient's record and generate the referral document.
Send the document to the connected printer.
Retrieve the printed document from the printer and verify that all details are correct.
Hand the printed referral document to the patient.
Ensure all patient data fields in the HIS are complete and saved.
Scan any paper documents (e.g., ID copy, insurance card) using the document scanner.
Attach the scanned images to the patient's electronic record in the HIS.
Mark the registration record as 'Archived' or 'Complete' in the system for compliance and future retrieval.
